title: Fleet lanes race on the shared .git/index.lock at ship (22 git-contention events/run) — serialize main-repo git mutations with a fleet-level lease; fast-forward already recovers, index.lock does NOT
summary: Observed live 2026-07-14 (width 3): 22 git-contention events in one run. TWO failure modes when N lanes ship to the shared repo concurrently: (1) FAST-FORWARD abort ('fatal: Not possible to fast-forward') — main moved because a sibling shipped first; this is HANDLED — orchestrator does contention backoff + GIT_FLEET_REBASE_NEEDED → rebase → re-audit → re-ship, and lanes DO land (fix-fleet-soak-red-ci recovered this way and PASSed cycle-806). (2) INDEX.LOCK race ('fatal: Unable to create .git/index.lock: File exists') — two lanes run a git mutation on the SAME .git at the same instant; this is NOT handled — the loser just errors and the cycle ends abnormally (preserved worktree). Root cause: lane ship/commit/merge operations mutate the ONE shared main-repo index without a mutual-exclusion lease. FIX (class): a fleet-level git-mutation lease (advisory lock file or in-process mutex owned by the supervisor) that serializes ALL main-repo index mutations across lanes — acquire before commit/merge/rebase/push, release after; a lane that can't acquire WAITS (bounded) instead of failing. This composes with the existing GIT_FLEET_REBASE_NEEDED rebase-recovery (fast-forward stays a rebase, not a lock error). Connects to merge-rung0 (trivial-rebase-carryforward would ALSO cut the re-audit cost of the fast-forward recoveries — carry the PASS verdict forward on a byte-identical rebase instead of re-auditing). NOT a release blocker (lanes recover), but it wastes lanes + adds ship latency at width>=2.
acceptance: RED: TestFleetShipLease_SerializesIndexMutations — two concurrent lane ships on one repo => second WAITS for the lease, no 'index.lock: File exists' error, both land
RED: TestFleetShipLease_FastForwardStillRebases — a base-moved ship still routes through GIT_FLEET_REBASE_NEEDED (lease does not mask the legitimate rebase path)
lease is bounded (timeout + WARN) so a dead holder cannot wedge the fleet; released on lane abort
git-contention index.lock events drop to ~0 in a width-3 soak; go test -race PASS; apicover clean
connects_to: merge-rung0-trivial-rebase-carryforward (carry-forward cuts the re-audit cost of fast-forward recoveries)
ship-window-lease (the demoted interim from merge-concurrency-2026 research)
source: live width-3 run 2026-07-14: 22 index.lock/fast-forward events; index.lock killed fix-fleet-soak-red-ci attempts (cycles 794/798/799/803) before it recovered+landed cycle-806
